body:
## Summary

Refactors the boundary-condition classification system so that the *matching scheme* concept is shared symmetrically between the boundary-normal-velocity condition and the value condition, as discussed in #5647 and the thread on #5646.

1. **Renames the `Open` classification to `NormalFlow`.** This restores the original historical name for this boundary condition (see #703, #848, #1140, #1156 — #1156 even links to `fill_halo_regions_normal_flow.jl`) and makes clear that the classification specifies the velocity component *normal* to a `Face`-located boundary. The renamed source file is `src/BoundaryConditions/fill_halo_regions_normal_flow.jl`.
2. **Adds a `scheme` to `Value`** (`Value{MS}`), mirroring `NormalFlow{MS}`. `ValueBoundaryCondition(val; scheme=nothing)` gains the keyword.
3. **Moves tracer (Center-located) `PerturbationAdvection` support** from the open path to `Value{<:PerturbationAdvection}`. Tracer open boundaries now use `ValueBoundaryCondition(c; scheme=PerturbationAdvection())`; boundary-normal velocities continue to use `OpenBoundaryCondition(u; scheme=…)`. This is the natural home for relaxing/radiating a scalar toward an exterior value, and gives downstream schemes (e.g. Breeze.jl's planned `AcousticRelaxation`) a place to live.

## Supersedes #5646

This PR also incorporates the #5646 bugfix (*apply tracer open boundary conditions in `NonhydrostaticModel`*): velocity and tracer halos are filled separately in the Nonhydrostatic `update_state!`. And because tracer radiation now lives on `Value` — which is **not** gated by `fill_open_bcs` — it fires during the regular halo fill, structurally fixing the bug. The #5646 regression test and the rewritten `tracer_outflow` validation are included (adapted to `ValueBoundaryCondition`), so this PR can supersede #5646.

## Breaking changes

- The `Open` classification is renamed to `NormalFlow` and `Open` is **no longer exported**. The public `OpenBoundaryCondition(val; scheme)` and `ImpenetrableBoundaryCondition()` constructors are unchanged.
- `Value` is now parametric (`Value{MS}`).
- Tracer `OpenBoundaryCondition(c; scheme=…)` must become `ValueBoundaryCondition(c; scheme=…)`.

## Also

- Fixes a latent invariant alias `const VBC = BoundaryCondition{Value}` → `BoundaryCondition{<:Value}` in `immersed_diffusive_fluxes.jl`, which would otherwise silently stop matching `Value` BCs once `Value` became parametric.
- Migrates tests, the `tracer_outflow` validation, and docs (`@ref`s + prose).

## Testing

Locally on CPU (Julia 1.12):
- `test_boundary_conditions.jl`: 275/275 pass
- `test_boundary_conditions_integration.jl`: 336/336 pass (includes the PA velocity/tracer tests, a new `NonhydrostaticModel` tracer-PA test, and the #5646 prescribed-value regression test)
- No new method ambiguities introduced (`detect_ambiguities` count is unchanged from `main` on this Julia version)
- `summary`/doctest output for `OpenBoundaryCondition{…}` is preserved

GPU jobs and doctests will be exercised by CI.

## Related

Closes #5647. Supersedes #5646. Builds on #5617, #5314. Background: #703, #848, #1140, #1156.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: (0.110.0) Rename `Open` → `NormalFlow` and add a `scheme` to `Value`

body: ## Summary Adds a `target_transport` option to `PerturbationAdvection` open boundary conditions that pins the net volume transport `∮u·dA` (m³/s) through a boundary to a prescribed value — letting users, for example, match an outflow to an inflow exactly. ## Changes - **`target_transport` keyword** on `PerturbationAdvection` (default `nothing`; existing behaviour unchanged). Accepts a number — a fixed transport — or any callable of the grid. - **`has_target_transport` / `get_target_transport` trait interface** — clean dispatch; external packages can extend these for custom schemes. - **`boundary_total_area(side, grid)`** — consolidated into `src/Models/boundary_mean.jl`, replacing duplicated per-side area helpers that previously lived in both model submodules. - **`NonhydrostaticModel`** — `enforce_net_zero_transport!` now does a two-pass correction: targeted boundaries are corrected first to hit their `target_transport`, then the remaining net imbalance is distributed over the non-targeted ("pool") boundaries to satisfy the zero-net-transport solvability condition. An error is raised when all open boundaries are targeted but the targets are inconsistent (nonzero net inflow). - **`HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel`** — the targeted correction is applied before the implicit free-surface solve, so the free surface stays consistent with the prescribed transport. - Updated all `OpenBoundaryCondition` / `Open` references to `NormalFlowBoundaryCondition` / `NormalFlow` following the rename in #5648. ## Status: exact for `NonhydrostaticModel`, approximate for `HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel` `target_transport` holds the boundary transport `∮u·dA` **exactly** for `NonhydrostaticModel`, where the correction is applied before the pressure solve. For `HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel` it is currently **approximate**: the correction is applied before the implicit free-surface solve — so the free surface and `η` stay consistent and there is no runaway — but the subsequent barotropic-mode correction perturbs the boundary velocity, so the achieved transport is close to, but not exactly, the target. A true fix — imposing the transport as a boundary condition inside the free-surface elliptic solve — can be done in a follow-up PR. ## Usage ```julia Q = U * boundary_total_area(:west, grid) # the transport to pin (m³/s) u_bcs = FieldBoundaryConditions( west = NormalFlowBoundaryCondition(U; scheme = PerturbationAdvection(; target_transport = Q)), east = NormalFlowBoundaryCondition(U; scheme = PerturbationAdvection(; target_transport = Q)), ) ``` ## Algorithm Each correction step, for a targeted boundary: `u[boundary] -= (∮u·dA − target) / boundary_area`, evaluated on the current grid. For `NonhydrostaticModel` the remaining net imbalance is then distributed uniformly over the non-targeted open boundaries. If all open boundaries are targeted and the targets do not sum to zero, an error is raised pointing the user to balance inflow/outflow targets or leave one pool boundary unspecified. ## Test plan - [x] `test_targeted_transport_achieved` — targeted west boundary reaches its transport target; pool east boundary compensates - [x] `test_targeted_transport_conservation` — two balanced targeted boundaries preserve `∫∇·u = 0` - [x] `test_targeted_south_transport_achieved` — targeted south (`v`) boundary - [x] `test_targeted_east_with_west_pool` — targeted east boundary with pool west boundary - [x] `test_open_boundary_condition_net_zero_transport`, `test_zero_inflow_open_boundary_conserves_mass`, `test_fixed_imposed_velocity_open_boundary_conserves_mass` — open-boundary regressions - [x] Targeted-transport tests run for `NonhydrostaticModel`, where the correction is exact; `HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel` coverage will be added with the true fix 🤖 Generated with Claude Code
title: Add target_transport to PerturbationAdvection for per-boundary transport control
